failure_asset.hypothesis_tested: fold-local train-only target-first tail-hazard analog memory with US100 M5 schedule replay
failure_asset.next_boundary: next C0005 work must change a meaningful analog model, feature, label, calibration, or trade-shape surface; do not retry R0004 by only nudging distance, target-hazard lift, stop-first rate, lower-tail, inner-outer gap, daily max entries, stop, target, hold, or session thresholds
failure_asset.reason_not_candidate: fold-isolated MT5 tick evidence completed with 8 losing folds, 1 profitable fold, negative total tick net PnL, and negative proxy plus MT5 tick profitability despite target-range entry frequency

supporting_lessons.summary: Initial target-first tail-hazard hard vetoes produced an empty schedule and MT5 rejected it as invalid_schedule_empty; repair changed eligibility to relaxed distance-only ranking before valid evidence was produced.
Closed-bar schedule replay parity passed, while tick execution worsened total net PnL by 6.10 versus logic and changed total trade count by -12; tick execution remains the economic surface.
Target-first tail-hazard analog memory achieved 7.389655 entries per active day but remained negative on 8 of 9 fold-isolated MT5 tick folds.

hypothesis_variant.summary: test whether fold-local target-first tail-hazard analog memory can separate target-before-stop opportunities from adverse-tail analogs after mean, contrast, and temporal-stability analog scoring failed
hypothesis_variant.variant_boundary: fourth C0005 true variant; changes label shape, feature representation, model score interpretation, and trade-logic interpretation toward asymmetric target-first probability, early favorable capture, and adverse-tail avoidance; not a neighbor-count, analog-distance, threshold, daily-count, stop, target, hold, session, or retry nudge of R0001 through R0003

notes: closed-bar MT5 mode is required for proxy-vs-EA logic parity
tick-mode MT5 is required for execution KPI management
fold-isolated MT5 tick and execution divergence are required before closeout
weak proxy evidence cannot skip MT5 validation
proxy must fit target-first tail-hazard analog memory only inside each rolling train window
proxy result cannot decide whether MT5 validation is attempted
proxy must report tail-risk diagnostics without creating selected claims
mechanical parity must compare scheduled entries, directions, exits, exit reasons, and position lifecycle behavior
tick-vs-closed-bar differences are execution divergence, not automatic logic failure
repair mechanical parity before any hypothesis judgment when mismatch is found

repair_records.reason: Initial target-first tail-hazard hard vetoes eliminated all candidates, producing an empty proxy schedule; MT5 schedule replay rejected the empty schedule with invalid_schedule_empty.
repair_records.repair_action: Use target-first tail-hazard score ranking with a relaxed distance-only eligibility filter, then rerun proxy and the same MT5 validation path.

feature.summary: continuous directional momentum, compression-release, range acceleration, wick/body pressure, prior adverse excursion, session-cycle, and spread-pressure features; no feature set is selected

label.summary: asymmetric directional label rewarding target-before-stop and early favorable capture while penalizing stop-first, large adverse tail, delayed target, and spread drag inside train folds; no label is selected

model.summary: fold-local target-first tail-hazard analog memory using train-only standardized continuous vectors, nearest-neighbor target-first labels, adverse-tail vetoes, and asymmetric hazard score calibration; no model family is selected

trade_logic.summary: fixed-lot discovery schedule selects highest target-first tail-hazard analog scores per active day with spacing and schedule replay for MT5 validation
